methodology_disclosures: Each feed is scored against that city's own trailing-12-month baseline: 50 = on its own baseline, >50 trending favorable, <50 unfavorable.
Not a cross-city ranking. A higher Pulse means a city is improving versus its own past, not that it is 'better' than another city.
Polarity is an editorial choice. Each feed declares which direction is favorable (permits up = good; crime / 311 backlog down = good); see each feed's polarity in the breakdown.
Data-continuity breaks can cause artificial jumps: Seattle PD's 2019 records-system change, LA's yearly dataset rotation, and SF's 2018 portal migration are known breakpoints.
Reporting lag: some feeds exclude the most recent days (Chicago crime excludes ~7 days), so 'Recent' is aligned to the last complete month.
Coverage honesty: when a city does not publish a pillar's feed, Pulse is computed on what is present and labeled 'N of 3 pillars' — missing data is never treated as zero.
City Context is a cross-city comparison of LEVELS, not a quality-of-life ranking and not a verdict on which city is 'best' to live in.
City Context and City Pulse are two separate numbers shown side by side and are never merged: Pulse measures a city against its own history (momentum); Context compares cities to each other (levels).

The composite is an editorial construction: each included KPI is equal-weighted by default. Included KPIs and their 'better' direction: median_income (higher=better), median_rent (lower=better), median_real_estate_taxes (lower=better), effective_property_tax_rate (lower=better), unemployment_rate (lower=better), aqi (lower=better).

median_home_value is intentionally EXCLUDED: its direction is ambiguous (a high value reads as desirable to owners but unaffordable to renters/buyers) and affordability is already captured by median_rent. It is shown in the Context strip but left out of the score.
Each KPI is min-max normalized to 0-100 across only the cities that report it (best value scores 100, worst scores 0; on a '-' KPI the scale is flipped so lower is better).

Coverage honesty: a city is scored on the mean of the KPIs it has and is never penalized or imputed for a missing KPI; a KPI reported by fewer than two cities can't be normalized and is dropped for everyone; a city with no usable KPI has no Context score.

cities.name: Chicago

feeds.label: Crimes 2001-present
feeds.note: excludes last ~7 days; latest complete month lags to prior month

disclosures: Crimes 2001-present: excludes last ~7 days; latest complete month lags to prior month

extended.label: Food Inspections
extended.note: CONFIRMED live (max inspection_date 2026-05-29; ~1.4k/mo). Polarity 0: inspection VOLUME is workload, not an outcome — more or fewer inspections is neither good nor bad on its own (the editorial signal is the Fail/Pass rate via the `results` column, not count). pillar_hint context. Latest complete month 2026-04.

extended.label: Traffic Crashes
extended.note: CONFIRMED live (max crash_date 2026-05-30; ~8-9k/mo, dense). Polarity -1: fewer reported crashes is favorable (Vision-Zero framing). pillar_hint public_safety but SUPPLEMENTARY — display alongside the crime pillar, not summed into it. Latest complete month 2026-04.

extended.label: Towed Vehicles
extended.note: CONFIRMED live (max tow_date 2026-05-31) BUT this is a ROLLING ~90-day inventory snapshot, not a historical series: min(tow_date)=2026-03-03, max=2026-05-31, total ~4.7k rows. CANNOT support a 12-month baseline or YoY — render as a recent-volume tile only (status insufficient_history in scorer output); do NOT z-score. Polarity 0: tow counts reflect enforcement/relocation activity, not safety or wellbeing. pillar_hint context.

extended.label: Business Licenses
extended.note: CONFIRMED live. license_start_date gives a clean recent flow (~2.0-2.3k/mo) ONCE future-dated rows are filtered: raw agg returns a large NULL bucket (~2.7k) plus forward-dated noise (2026-08/09/11), so filter `license_start_date IS NOT NULL AND license_start_date < <next-month>` before bucketing. date_issued is an even cleaner alternative (~2.3-2.7k/mo, no future noise, max 2026-05-29) and mirrors the backbone Building-Permits issue_date framing — use it if the future-date filter on license_start_date proves fragile. Polarity +1: new/renewed licenses up-is-favorable. pillar_hint development_economy. Latest complete month 2026-04.

feeds.label: DOB Permit Issuance
feeds.note: issuance_date is TEXT -> date_trunc_ym fails. Bucket via substring(issuance_date,7,4)||'-'||substring(issuance_date,1,2) and filter IS NOT NULL, OR use dobrundate (real calendar_date, ~1-day proxy).

extended.label: DOHMH Restaurant Inspections
extended.note: CONFIRMED live (~6.5-9.3k/mo, dense). MUST filter placeholder rows: ~3,434 records carry inspection_date='1900-01-01' (never-inspected / new establishments) — filter `inspection_date > '1901-01-01'` before bucketing or date_trunc_ym buries a huge 1900 bucket. Polarity 0: inspection VOLUME is workload, not outcome (signal = critical_flag / score, not count). pillar_hint context. Latest complete month 2026-04.

extended.label: Motor Vehicle Collisions - Crashes
extended.note: CONFIRMED live (max crash_date 2026-05-26; ~6-6.7k/mo, dense). NYC Vision Zero crash file. Polarity -1: fewer collisions is favorable. pillar_hint public_safety, SUPPLEMENTARY (display alongside the NYPD-complaints pillar, not summed in). Latest complete month 2026-04.

cities.name: Los Angeles

feeds.label: MyLA311 Cases
feeds.note: RESOLVED: LA retired the 'MyLA311 Service Request Data {year}' series ~Aug 2025 (old h73f-gn57=2025 is dead for current data). Current product = 'MyLA311 Cases {year}'. 2026 = 2cy6-i7zn (date col createddate, live, ~185k/mo). Baseline = 73a2-6ar5 ('MyLA311 Cases March 2025 to December 2025'). Resolve current-year by catalog search: GET data.lacity.org/api/catalog/v1?q=MyLA311%20Cases -> item titled 'MyLA311 Cases {currentYear}'. Do NOT auto-build '...Service Request Data {year}' IDs.

feeds.label: LAPD NIBRS Offenses
feeds.note: UNION SEAM CONFIRMED: baseline y8y3-fqfu complete through 2025-12; live k7nn-b2ep dense from 2026-01. Both keyed on date_occ. Clip baseline to trailing window - both tables hold a few dirty pre-2010 dates from data-entry errors; ignore raw MIN(date_occ). Legacy 2nrs-mtv8 frozen - never poll.

cities.name: Miami (Miami-Dade County)

feeds.label: Miami-Dade 311
feeds.note: STALE: this is the 2023 yearly snapshot (ticket_created_date_time range 2023-01-01 -> 2024-01-01). County 311 is published only as frozen per-year snapshots ('311 Service Requests - Miami-Dade County - {year}'); latest = 2023. NO current County 311 feed exists (alternates b74b1dd0=2018 max, a6b9c3e3=OBJECTID-only, 7cc10915=City-of-Miami footprint max 2024-08). opendata.miamidade.gov is the same ArcGIS Hub org (no Socrata BETA found). Resolve latest snapshot by catalog title pattern; use created_year_month (int yyyymm) for bucketing. Expect Miami Services = stale in P0; Miami likely ships 2-of-3 pillars.

feeds.label: MDC Building Permit
feeds.note: LIVE: ISSUDATE range 1982-09-22 -> 2026-05-21 (current). Plenty for 12-mo baseline + YoY. No date_trunc in ArcGIS: bucket via monthly date-range loop (where=ISSUDATE >= timestamp 'YYYY-MM-01' AND < next-month, returnCountOnly=true) or paginate (resultOffset, maxRecordCount 2000) + bucket client-side. Filter ISSUDATE IS NOT NULL.

feeds.label: FBI CDE (fallback)
feeds.note: ORI FL0130000 resolved keyless from agencies endpoint. Spec's 'MDSO' = this county PD entry (county PD->Sheriff transition not yet renamed in CDE). City-of-Miami-proper PD = FL0130600 if scope flips. DATA series requires free api.data.gov key (FBI_CDE_API_KEY) as ?API_KEY=...; agencies list is keyless. NIBRS from 2022-01 -> 12-mo baseline+YoY feasible once keyed. P0: Safety = not_published/pending until key set.

disclosures: Footprint = Miami-Dade County (not the City of Miami). County 311 excludes City-of-Miami-proper requests.
